svg.title: Paged KV cache removes a concurrency waste a single-user local engine does not have, until the prefix tree branches
svg.desc: A memory-layout schematic on a deep forest-green background. The top band, titled what paging was built to remove, KV waste under concurrent serving, shows two horizontal memory bars. The upper bar, reserve every request to its max length, is mostly a hatched copper region marked reserved and fragmented, with only a small gold segment at the left marked actual tokens, labeled only 20.4 to 38.2 percent holds real tokens, vLLM Figure 2. The lower bar, PagedAttention, fixed blocks committed on demand, is a dense row of small gold blocks with thin gaps and a tiny stub at the end, labeled near-zero waste under 4 percent and 2 to 4 times serving throughput, the kernel walks a block table each step. A small legend shows gold equals active tokens, hatch equals reserved or wasted, mint equals shared prefix. The bottom band, titled the local case, batch of one and the prefix tree that breaks it, has two halves. The left half, one sequence and one contiguous arena, is a single gold bar filled about sixty percent with an arrow grows as decode extends, labeled no inter-request fragmentation to fight, attention reads contiguous KV, zinc sizes this from vLLM block-budget math at 0.85 utilization. The right half, a branching conversation with RadixAttention, is a small tree: a mint shared root prompt node and a mint shared chain that splits into three gold leaf branches, labeled siblings must share the parent KV, storing each branch contiguously would duplicate the shared prefix so block-granular sharing returns. A footer notes the waste and throughput figures are from the vLLM PagedAttention paper, Kwon and coauthors SOSP 2023, the kernel-overhead framing is from vAttention, Prabhu and coauthors 2024, and proportions are illustrative.
svg.text: Paging removes a waste a single-user decode does not have
KV cache memory management on a 32 GB Radeon AI PRO R9700, single-user decode versus high-concurrency serving.
What paging was built to remove: KV waste under concurrent serving
Reserve every request to its max length (serving, pre-paging)
actual tokens
reserved + internal fragmentation
Only 20.4 to 38.2 percent holds real tokens; the rest is reservation and fragmentation.
PagedAttention: fixed blocks committed on demand
Near-zero waste (under 4%) and 2 to 4 times serving throughput, paid for by a block-table lookup in the attention kernel each step.
The local case: batch of one, and the prefix tree that brings the problem back
One sequence, one contiguous arena
tokens so far
grows as
decode extends
No inter-request fragmentation to fight, and the
attention kernel reads one contiguous KV buffer.
zinc default: vLLM's block-budget math, allocated as a
contiguous arena at 0.85 device-memory utilization.
A branching conversation (RadixAttention)
shared system prompt
shared chat prefix
branch A
branch B
branch C
Siblings must share the parent's KV. Storing each branch
contiguously would duplicate the shared prefix, so
block-granular sharing is back on the table.
Waste and throughput figures from the vLLM PagedAttention paper (Kwon et al., SOSP 2023). Kernel-overhead framing from vAttention
(Prabhu et al., 2024). Bar and tree proportions are schematic and illustrate the allocation mechanism, not a measured layout.
